svg.text: The movement law holds in every cell of the judge factorial:
one frozen number (0.833) predicts next-round drift whatever the judge prompt or judge model
Held-out test - 24 supplier-removed em750 self-loop runs (judge prompt x judge model, seeds 41-46); constants frozen
from earlier fits (C=0.96, K=0.833), never refit on this data.
candid judge prompt + self judge model
neutral judge prompt + self judge model
candid judge prompt + base judge model
neutral judge prompt + base judge model
1. Movement law: 0.833 x gap -> next-round drift
Same frozen law, all four judge
2. Factorization: 0.96 x rho x sigma -> this-round gap
frozen prediction = 0.96 x rho x sigma

Points: Panel 1 n=72 round-to-round steps (all judge cells 18 each); Panel 2 n=77 rounds (rho defined). x is the FROZEN prediction, y the realized value; dashed line is the identity, so a point on the

line means the frozen law hit exactly with no free parameter. Refit slopes are diagnostics, not used to draw the line. Source: experiments/ablation_unit_law.json (scorer analysis_ablation_unit_law.py;
conventions from analysis_spread_util_unified.py). Frozen C=0.96, K=0.833 fit on simple_model_rollout.json / spread_util_unified.json.
